title: Cirrus: Update CI VM images to F37beta
body:
Fixes: #15349
Depends on: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15871 https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15890
Image build PR: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/195

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
@umohnani8 this PR also stubs-in a spot for you to setup and run your kube tests.  I added it to run after the system-tests, but am not married to the order if you want it to run sooner.  However, since this PR brings in a new Fedora version as well, it might get hairy depending on how CI is feeling today :smile: If it goes south, you're welcome to split out only the kube-additions into a separate PR using image-suffix `c5167094042984448` from https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/192  In other words, those images are the good 'ole F35 & F36.  The important diffs for this are (edited):

* [`.cirrus.yml`](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15760/files#diff-62587956f943bb2503db7bc6dd27d0d888074a1c0ecaab3f570ad611aff0f7bbR844-R861) and [this line](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15760/files#diff-62587956f943bb2503db7bc6dd27d0d888074a1c0ecaab3f570ad611aff0f7bbR1060).
* [`contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh`](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15760/files#diff-f3866c83e5fdf3650df2ad5b9c0c18efaa71891d23675732cc931e90fd2b10faR307-R313)
* [`contrib/cirrus/runner.sh`](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15760/files#diff-db575befc56ec85f83b824430b9b62e20eb19d08b351dc743db26848639896a6R136-R142)

As always, ping me if you need help.

Thanks @cevich! 

Is this still WIP or can we get this merged in?

> Is this still WIP or can we get this merged in?

That's the 10-billion dollar question.  Historically, bringing in a new OS version into CI is fraught with "challenges".  Sometimes though, it goes smoothly.  So this PR is me rolling the dice and crossing my fingers.

Initially, [the validate failures](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6152769441628160) don't give me confidence :cry:
Looks like the package name for `io/ioutil` changed with the Go version bump, maybe updating the package name in the files it is complaining about will fix the issue.

> maybe updating the package name in the files it is complaining about will fix the issue.

You're talking to a guy who dreams in bash :grinning: 

I'm going to take a wild-guess that we're going to hit that same error in every single containers-repo :cry:

from rerun with terminal
```
# rpm -q golang
golang-1.19.1-1.fc37.x86_64
```

I'm now hitting the `can't load fmt` issue open at https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/issues/3107 both locally and on cirrus . Seems like there's a workaround at https://github.com/go-critic/go-critic/issues/1126#issuecomment-1212554778 but didn't work for me.

Crud, so @containers/podman-maintainers we're potentially going to see this pop up everywhere then until there's a fix and we bump all our `golangci-lint` version variables in every repo. :disappointed:
@cevich can we separate the fedora version bump from the addition of minikube so we don't block on that? I can open another PR with just the minikube changes

> I can open another PR with just the minikube changes

Yes absolutely, please do this, and poke me if you run into trouble.  [My comment above links to the minikube-enablement bits you need](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15760#issuecomment-1244533343).  Pay careful attention to the image ID called out there, since everything built after that is using F36/F37.
Force-push: Removed [the mini-kube addition](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/cdc0ba61e66cccf96a9f8059e60607f0086e255c).
Opened issue for [netavark: failed to delete if podman4: Received a netlink error message Operation not supported (os error 95)](https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/426)
Force-push w/ freshly built images: These should use CNI in F36 instead of Netavark, which bypasses the issue I ran into. :crossed_fingers:

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL, [CNI is broken in various ways](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5058027626692608/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html) (ex. log).  Likely nobody noticed because F35 testing (where CNI was configured) has been disabled for quite some time.

I'm assuming we want F37 brought into widespread testing (here and elsewhere) fairly quickly.   Do we care about testing CNI in F36 or at all?

* If the answer is "no", then should I go through and disable all the CNI tests and/or build the F36 images to run with netavark instead?

* If the answer is "yes", then I need some guidance on how to proceed past these failures so the system tests can run (and probably fail differently).  In other words, should I really open up 8 separate issues on these failures?  Is there some "easy-button" to assist, that I may shove this bolder up the hill faster?   Did I screw something up when building these F36 images perhaps?  

I'm suspecting the answer is some form of "yes", but "I dunno, I just work here buddy" :grinning:
All failures look dns related, is dnsname installed? It should be part of the `podman-plugins` rpm.

> It should be part of the podman-plugins rpm.

Ahh this is helpful, thanks. It's suppose to be installed, but perhaps the mechanism broke, I'll double check.

Force-push: Updated setup w/ addition of explicit install of `podman-plugins` when CNI networking should be used.  How the tests were passing (a while ago) in previous F35 images (with CNI) is a mystery.  I'd guess that maybe they were coming in via some dependency resolution :man_shrugging:
Force-push: Brand-new fresh images.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL, these are *new* [APIv2 test failures](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5930286260158464/html/apiv2-podman-fedora-37-rootless-host.log.html) (and new VM images) as of today.  Yesterday the APIv2 test was passing (with slightly older VM images).

I'm seeing [messages like this all over the place](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4763373341507584/html/sys-remote-fedora-37-rootless-host.log.html):

    Error: failed to make pod: unable to create pod cgroup for pod <blah>: creating cgroup user.slice/user-
    libpod_pod_<blah>.slice: Unit user-libpod_pod_<blah>.slice already exists.
Filed #16107 for rootless failures. f36 failures seem to be cni-related.

Thanks @edsantiago.  Looking at the issue I do remember briefly searching around for similar reports before getting distracted by a squirrel or an IRC question or something :grinning: 

The problem seems significant enough I'd rather not skip all the affected tests (and it would be a lot of work).  I'll try rebasing and re-running in a few minutes, maybe the API test failures will magically get fixed :grin:
Update: I re-ran a F36 system test task, all the others appear related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16107
@cevich Any update on this?

rootless f36 is [thinking that it's netavark](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6369112698912768?logs=main#L20):
```
[+0008s] # Arch:amd64 [...]  Cgroups:v2+systemd Net:netavark
```
I think we need a `CI_DESIRED_NETWORKING` mechanism like the `RUNTIME` one added in #14912.

I have an f36 VM but cannot find any way to make it CNI. I've tried `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/*`, I obviously created `/etc/cni/net.d/87-blahblah`, tried rebooting, nothing makes it go to CNI.
The only way to force cni/netavark is via containers.conf network_backend option or --network-backend option for each podman command.
OK, we need #15413 then.

Status: Finally Green!

I'm rolling up one final fresh set of images so I can remove a workaround Jason did for the new windows tests.  I'll slap that in here when it's ready and then (:crossed_fingers:) this will be good to go (finally :sweat_smile:)

CI is green, I'm OK with merging this since it conflicts with my network-check PR. I will fix the comments there.

/lgtm

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ugh...but...but....there was one more thing I needed to add...sigh...https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16396

| ---- | --------            | ----     |    ----: |   -----: | --------: |
| int  | fedora-36           | root     |    29:41 |    30:06 |     29:21 |
| int  | fedora-37           | root     |    26:19 |    31:16 |     26:01 |
| int  | fedora-36           | rootless |    27:00 |          |
| int  | fedora-37           | rootless |    25:23 |          |
| sys  | fedora-36           | root     |    24:47 |    16:36 |
| sys  | fedora-37           | root     |    25:14 |    16:26 |
| sys  | fedora-37-aarch64   | root     |    29:45 |    20:08 |
| sys  | fedora-36           | rootless |    23:39 |          |
| sys  | fedora-37           | rootless |    22:37 |    13:50 |
| bud  | fedora-37           | root     |    25:13 |    25:16 |
